indication: Chronic low back pain, numbness and tingling in bilateral lower extremities, evaluate for degenerative disease
technique: AP and lateral radiographs of the lumbar spine were obtained.

findings.alignment: Normal lumbar lordosis. No spondylolisthesis. Vertebral body heights are maintained.
findings.vertebralBodies: Mild anterior osteophyte formation at L3-L4, L4-L5, and L5-S1. No compression fracture. No lytic or blastic lesions.
findings.discSpaces: Moderate disc space narrowing at L4-L5 and L5-S1. Mild disc space narrowing at L3-L4. Vacuum disc phenomenon at L5-S1.
findings.facetJoints: Bilateral facet hypertrophy at L4-L5 and L5-S1.
findings.softTissues: Atherosclerotic calcification of the abdominal aorta. No paraspinal soft tissue mass.
findings.pelvis: Bilateral hip joints demonstrate mild degenerative changes.

impression: Multilevel degenerative disc disease, most pronounced at L4-L5 and L5-S1 with moderate disc space narrowing.
Facet arthropathy at L4-L5 and L5-S1.
recommendations: If symptoms persist or worsen, MRI of the lumbar spine is recommended to evaluate for spinal canal stenosis and nerve root compression
comparison: No prior lumbar spine radiographs available for comparison.
